issue_title: Make llama.cpp an optional LLM backend (introduce LlmBackend abstraction)
issue_body:
## Background

GenieClaw's LLM client is currently hard-wired to `llama-server`'s OpenAI-compatible API at `/v1/chat/completions`. See `crates/genie-core/src/llm/client.rs:6`:

```rust
/// Client for llama.cpp `--server` OpenAI-compatible API.
```

The repo already calls this out as a transitional adapter that should be peeled off when `genie-ai-runtime` is split out:

- README.md L68 — *"transitional `llama.cpp` support while `genie-ai-runtime` is not yet split out"*
- ARCHITECTURE.md L81 — *"`llama.cpp` OpenAI-compatible client → `genie-ai-runtime` client. Keep the client boundary narrow and model/runtime assumptions explicit."*

This issue promotes that boundary from "soft TODO" to a real backend abstraction, with `llama.cpp` becoming **one option among several** rather than the only path. The companion issue (flipping the default to `genie-ai-runtime`) depends on this one landing first.

## Proposed design

Introduce an `LlmClient` trait (or enum) in `crates/genie-core/src/llm/` that the rest of the codebase consumes:

```rust
#[async_trait]
pub trait LlmClient: Send + Sync {
    async fn health(&self) -> bool;
    async fn chat(&self, req: ChatRequest) -> Result<ChatResponse>;
    async fn chat_stream(
        &self,
        req: ChatRequest,
        on_token: impl FnMut(&str),
    ) -> Result<String>;
}

pub enum LlmBackend {
    LlamaCpp(LlamaCppClient),          // current llama-server adapter
    GenieAiRuntime(GenieRuntimeClient), // companion issue
    // future: Triton, vLLM-aarch64, etc.
}
```

The existing `LlmClient` struct in `client.rs:11` gets renamed to `LlamaCppClient` and moved to `crates/genie-core/src/llm/llama_cpp.rs`. The rest of the code consumes the trait, not a concrete type.

## Configuration shape

In `geniepod.toml`:

```toml
[services.llm]
backend = "llama_cpp"   # or "genie_ai_runtime"; default stays llama_cpp here
                        # (the default flip is the companion issue)
url     = "http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions"

# Backend-specific knobs go under a backend-named subtable:
[services.llm.llama_cpp]
systemd_unit = "genie-llm.service"

[services.llm.genie_ai_runtime]
systemd_unit = "genie-ai-runtime.service"
```

For backwards compatibility, when `backend` is absent we infer `llama_cpp` from the legacy keys (`services.llm.url`, `services.llm.systemd_unit`) for one release cycle.

## Files touched

- `crates/genie-core/src/llm/client.rs` — split into `mod.rs` (trait) + `llama_cpp.rs` (current impl)
- `crates/genie-core/src/llm/retry.rs` — generic over the trait
- `crates/genie-common/src/config.rs:148` — add `backend` enum field on `[services.llm]`
- `crates/genie-core/src/main.rs` — backend selection at startup
- `crates/genie-core/src/voice_loop.rs:152` — `llm.health()` already trait-shaped
- `crates/genie-core/src/lib.rs` — re-exports
- `deploy/config/geniepod.toml` and `geniepod.dev.toml` — add commented-out `backend = "..."` examples
- `deploy/systemd/genie-llm.service` — unchanged, but now optional in the deploy story

## Feature flags

Cargo features gate which backend code is compiled:

```toml
[features]
default      = ["llama-cpp"]
llama-cpp    = []
genie-ai-runtime = []
```

`make jetson` enables both; `make release` for dev machines defaults to `llama-cpp` only.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `LlmClient` is a trait; `client.rs:6` doc comment no longer says "Client for llama.cpp"
- [ ] `geniepod.toml` accepts `[services.llm].backend` and the right client is constructed
- [ ] `genie-ctl`, `genie-health`, `genie-governor` continue to work against both backends
- [ ] CI matrix exercises both backends (mock for the runtime if cross-compile in CI is impractical)
- [ ] Legacy config keys still work for one release cycle (deprecation warning logged)
- [ ] ARCHITECTURE.md L81 entry resolved
- [ ] README.md L68 wording updated from "transitional" to "default with `genie-ai-runtime` opt-in"

## Why this is one of the cheapest moves we can make

The boundary is already narrow — `LlmClient` is constructed once in `main.rs` and threaded through. The work is mostly trait extraction + config plumbing, no behavioral change. Risk is low; payoff is that the companion issue (defaulting to `genie-ai-runtime`) becomes a one-line config change.

## Related

- Companion issue: defaulting `genie-ai-runtime` once parity is confirmed.
- Upstream: https://github.com/GeniePod/genie-ai-runtime
